insights.title: Staged Scene Detection
insights.summary: An orderly scene can be deliberately arranged to project innocence.
insights.transfer_rule: Do not treat surface calm as proof of natural events.

cases.title: The Silent Room
cases.summary: A room appears calm and undisturbed, yet several details suggest the scene may have been curated. Apply the three-layer protocol.
evidence: A chair is slightly pulled back from the table.
A notebook lies open on the table.
A glass of water is half full.
detective_questions: What are all possible reasons the window is open?
Was the notebook abandoned suddenly or intentionally left open?
Why is the chair not aligned with the table?
What explains the inward movement of the curtains?
analysis_protocol: For each major clue, list 2–3 explanations.
Delay commitment until contradictions are mapped.
scenarios: A person exited through the window.
A person is still hiding inside the room.
cases.hidden_truth: The room's order should not be trusted at face value.
cases.false_narrative: The room is calm, so nothing deceptive happened.

evidence_meta.text: A chair is slightly pulled back from the table.
A notebook lies open on the table.
A glass of water is half full.

inference_traps.description: Open window does not by itself prove entry/exit through the window; it may be part of staging.
Surface calm is not evidence that nothing happened; orderly scenes can be fabricated.
trap_keywords: room is calm so nothing happened

required_concept_rules.name: Scene detail grounding
required_concept_rules.description: Reasoning references actual scene elements with their observed states.

required_concept_rules.name: Staging recognition
required_concept_rules.description: Recognizes the scene may be deliberately arranged despite calm surface.

forbidden_patterns.description: Accepting surface calm as genuine without questioning.
